title: Cleghornia chinensis

It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties that may help protect against various diseases, including cardiovascular and neurodegenerative conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cleghornia chinensis include limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat digestive and respiratory issues.
